Home / Break-Even Calculator

Break-Even Calculator

Calculate when you will break even — with and without monthly revenue growth. See cumulative P&L at 3, 6, 12, and 24 months.

Scenarios:

How to Use This Tool

1
Enter your total monthly operating costs.
2
Enter your current or expected monthly revenue.
3
Enter your total initial investment before launch.
4
Enter your estimated monthly growth rate (0 if revenue is flat).
5
Compare the flat vs growth break-even timelines.
6
Review the cumulative P&L outlook at 3, 6, 12, and 24 months.
7
If growth shaves months off break-even, focus energy on sustaining that growth rate.

Frequently Asked Questions

What counts as initial investment?

Any upfront costs before launch: development, design tools, legal fees, equipment, initial ads, and living expenses during the build phase. Include everything spent before your first dollar of revenue.

How should I estimate my monthly growth rate?

If you have existing data, use the last 3-month average. For pre-launch, SaaS typically grows 5-15% monthly early on. Be conservative — better to be pleasantly surprised than to plan around overly optimistic numbers.

Should I include living expenses in monthly costs?

Yes, if this is your full-time venture. Otherwise you are calculating business break-even, not personal break-even — and the latter is what determines whether you can keep going.

How does growth rate change break-even math?

Without growth, each month adds the same net profit. With 10% monthly growth, revenue compounds — by month 12 you generate ~3× more than month 1. Even modest growth rates dramatically accelerate break-even for subscription businesses.

How can I speed up my break-even?

Launch an MVP sooner, offer pre-sales, add a consulting layer, or reduce scope. Time to market is the biggest lever — every month you delay launch is another month of costs without revenue.

Results

Copy All
Example

📊 Break-Even Analysis

⏱️ Break-Even Timeline • Initial Investment: $5,000 • Monthly Costs: $500/mo • Monthly Revenue: $1,000/mo • Flat revenue: 10 months • With 10.0% growth: 7 months (3 months faster) 🟢 Excellent! Breaking even in under 6 months is a very healthy trajectory. 📈 Cumulative P&L Outlook • Month 3: −$1,690 • Month 6: +$2,716 ✅ • Month 12: +$21,384 ✅ • Month 24: +$89,497 ✅
